title: The Movie Star Ranking: 35 Under 35
format: Sean and Amanda's original 35-under-35 movie star ranking: English speakers only, nobody over 34, and a coin-flip finish between Emma Stone and Margot Robbie.

segments.heading: Tough cuts and honorable mentions
segments.note: who almost made it, and who got left off entirely

films.title: The Town
films.note: Blake Lively, an honorable mention who wasn't even up for debate.

films.title: The Farewell
films.note: Awkwafina's breakout; the hosts will wait and see where she takes it.

films.title: Terminator: Dark Fate
films.note: Mackenzie Davis, a hard cut Sean says might've made it if the movie had worked.

films.title: Pitch Perfect
films.note: Anna Kendrick, cut for a rough last five years otherwise.

films.title: Trolls World Tour
films.note: Anna Kendrick; Amanda's line: if they're doing a Trolls episode, it's not happening here.

films.title: Call Me by Your Name
films.note: Armie Hammer, cut; the hosts wonder where he's been since.

films.title: Death on the Nile
films.note: Armie Hammer's next, delayed to that fall.

films.title: True Grit
films.note: Hailee Steinfeld, cut in favor of Haley Lou Richardson.

films.title: Bumblebee
films.note: Hailee Steinfeld.

films.title: The Hunt
films.note: Betty Gilpin, cut; the hosts just talked about this one earlier that week.

films.title: Stuber
films.note: Betty Gilpin, mostly playing the girlfriend role.

films.title: The Grudge
films.note: Betty Gilpin, in what Amanda calls a very bad remake.

films.title: Luce
films.note: Kelvin Harrison Jr., a hard cut with a bright future ahead.

films.title: Waves
films.note: Kelvin Harrison Jr.

films.title: The King of Staten Island
films.note: Pete Davidson, then an untitled Judd Apatow movie; cut for being best known for TV and stand-up.

films.title: The Suicide Squad
films.note: Pete Davidson's other reason he might make next year's list.

segments.heading: The international pick and the kids list
segments.note: one non-English-language star apiece, plus the under-21 shortlist

films.title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
films.note: Amanda's international pick, Adele Haenel: 'the most arresting presence I have ever seen in a movie.'

films.title: Parasite
films.note: Sean's international pick, Choi Woo-shik, as the son.

films.title: Okja
films.note: Choi Woo-shik, another Bong Joon-ho film.

films.title: Train to Busan
films.note: Choi Woo-shik; the zombie thriller fans got mad wasn't on the horror decade list.

films.title: Time to Hunt
films.note: Choi Woo-shik's next, on Netflix.

films.title: Jojo Rabbit
films.note: Archie Yates and Thomasin McKenzie, both on the kids list.

films.title: Honey Boy
films.note: Noah Jupe, on the kids list.

films.title: A Quiet Place
films.note: Noah Jupe.

films.title: A Quiet Place Part II
films.note: Noah Jupe.

films.title: The Goldfinch
films.note: Finn Wolfhard, on the kids list.

films.title: It
films.note: Finn Wolfhard.

films.title: It Chapter Two
films.note: Finn Wolfhard.

films.title: Room
films.note: Jacob Tremblay, on the kids list; he was at the Oscars but not nominated.

films.title: Good Boys
films.note: Jacob Tremblay, star of 2019's biggest comedy hit.

films.title: The Fault in Our Stars
films.note: No. 35 Ansel Elgort: the signature weepy of the decade, and it works on Amanda every time.

films.title: Baby Driver
films.note: No. 35 Ansel Elgort: the Edgar Wright movie, his most beloved credit.

films.title: The Goldfinch
films.note: No. 35 Ansel Elgort: high-profile, not good.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: No. 35 Ansel Elgort: the lead in Spielberg's take, the most anticipated prestige title on the horizon.

films.title: Straight Outta Compton
films.note: No. 34 O'Shea Jackson Jr.: played his own father, Ice Cube.

films.title: Den of Thieves
films.note: No. 34 O'Shea Jackson Jr.

films.title: Long Shot
films.note: No. 34 O'Shea Jackson Jr.: steals scenes from Seth Rogen and Charlize Theron.

films.title: Just Mercy
films.note: No. 34 O'Shea Jackson Jr.: moving, not funny, part of a death-row trio Sean's thought about since.

films.title: Beast
films.note: No. 33 Jessie Buckley.

films.title: Wild Rose
films.note: No. 33 Jessie Buckley: a 'who is that?' performance Sean's been shouting about since last April.

films.title: Dolittle
films.note: No. 33 Jessie Buckley: sleeps under the covers for most of the film.

films.title: I'm Thinking of Ending Things
films.note: No. 33 Jessie Buckley: the Kaufman adaptation of Sean's favorite recent novel, up next.

films.title: Crazy Rich Asians
films.note: No. 32 Henry Golding: went from travel-show host to overnight star within months.

films.title: A Simple Favor
films.note: No. 32 Henry Golding: Sean walked out of it; it still made $100M+ internationally.

films.title: The Gentlemen
films.note: No. 32 Henry Golding.

films.title: Snake Eyes: G.I. Joe Origins
films.note: No. 32 Henry Golding: the only other project on his slate, as the title character.

films.title: Emma.
films.note: No. 31 Anya Taylor-Joy: brings real haughtiness to the role.

films.title: The Witch
films.note: No. 31 Anya Taylor-Joy: the mysterious, entrancing performance that started it.

films.title: Split
films.note: No. 31 Anya Taylor-Joy.

films.title: Glass
films.note: No. 31 Anya Taylor-Joy.

films.title: The New Mutants
films.note: No. 31 Anya Taylor-Joy: one of two big titles coming this year.

films.title: Last Night in Soho
films.note: No. 31 Anya Taylor-Joy: Edgar Wright's next.

films.title: Slumdog Millionaire
films.note: No. 30 Dev Patel.

films.title: Lion
films.note: No. 30 Dev Patel: Oscar-nominated; Chris Ryan does a DJ Khaled bit about it.

films.title: The Personal History of David Copperfield
films.note: No. 30 Dev Patel: Armando Iannucci's Dickens adaptation, the pick that inspired this whole episode.

films.title: The Green Knight
films.note: No. 30 Dev Patel: the David Lowery A24 film Sean expects a 'moment' around.

films.title: Manchester by the Sea
films.note: No. 29 Lucas Hedges: going toe-to-toe with Casey Affleck, one of the decade's great revelations.

films.title: Lady Bird
films.note: No. 29 Lucas Hedges.

films.title: Waves
films.note: No. 29 Lucas Hedges.

films.title: Honey Boy
films.note: No. 29 Lucas Hedges: an interesting, if imperfect, bid for false masculinity.

films.title: Let Them All Talk
films.note: No. 29 Lucas Hedges: Soderbergh's next.

films.title: An Education
films.note: No. 28 Carey Mulligan: her breakout, still wonderful on rewatch.

films.title: The Great Gatsby
films.note: No. 28 Carey Mulligan: Daisy, in what Amanda calls basically a Lana Del Rey song.

films.title: Wildlife
films.note: No. 28 Carey Mulligan: Paul Dano's directing debut; both hosts think she's tremendous in it.

films.title: Inside Llewyn Davis
films.note: No. 28 Carey Mulligan: the same caustic register she reaches for in Wildlife.

films.title: Promising Young Woman
films.note: No. 28 Carey Mulligan: Emerald Fennell's post-#MeToo revenge fantasy, seen at Sundance.

films.title: Everybody Wants Some!!
films.note: No. 27 Glen Powell: the already-underrated Linklater college-baseball movie.

films.title: Hidden Figures
films.note: No. 27 Glen Powell: plays an astronaut, heartthrob role No. 1.

films.title: Set It Up
films.note: No. 27 Glen Powell: one of the most beloved Netflix movies ever made, opposite Zoey Deutch.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: No. 27 Glen Powell: one of the stars, whenever it comes out.

films.title: Us
films.note: No. 26 Yahya Abdul-Mateen II.

films.title: Aquaman
films.note: No. 26 Yahya Abdul-Mateen II: the bad guy.

films.title: Candyman
films.note: No. 26 Yahya Abdul-Mateen II: the Jordan Peele-produced remake.

films.title: The Trial of the Chicago 7
films.note: No. 26 Yahya Abdul-Mateen II: playing Bobby Seale, in Sorkin's next.

films.title: The Matrix Resurrections
films.note: No. 26 Yahya Abdul-Mateen II: horror, prestige Oscar movie, tentpole, the whole ladder, back to back.

films.title: Support the Girls
films.note: No. 25 Haley Lou Richardson: the performance that made Amanda Google her a second time.

films.title: Columbus
films.note: No. 25 Haley Lou Richardson: a Sean recommendation Amanda sought out months later and loved.

films.title: The Edge of Seventeen
films.note: No. 25 Haley Lou Richardson: a pretty big supporting part.

films.title: Unpregnant
films.note: No. 25 Haley Lou Richardson: a slightly more serious part.

films.title: After Yang
films.note: No. 25 Haley Lou Richardson: Kogonada's follow-up to Columbus, opposite Colin Farrell.

films.title: Moonlight
films.note: No. 24 Janelle Monae: an authentically great performance.

films.title: Hidden Figures
films.note: No. 24 Janelle Monae.

films.title: The Glorias
films.note: No. 24 Janelle Monae: plays Dorothy Pittman Hughes, seen at Sundance.

films.title: Antebellum
films.note: No. 24 Janelle Monae: the glossy Lionsgate horror movie that puts her at the actual center of the frame; early word is good.

films.title: Rabbit Hole
films.note: No. 23 Miles Teller: opposite Nicole Kidman, years before Whiplash.

films.title: The Spectacular Now
films.note: No. 23 Miles Teller.

films.title: Whiplash
films.note: No. 23 Miles Teller: a Ringer favorite since almost a decade ago.

films.title: Solo: A Star Wars Story
films.note: No. 23 Miles Teller: famously didn't get the Han Solo role; both hosts think he'd have killed it.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: No. 23 Miles Teller.

films.title: Fifty Shades of Grey
films.note: No. 22 Dakota Johnson: what she's best known for.

films.title: A Bigger Splash
films.note: No. 22 Dakota Johnson.

films.title: Suspiria
films.note: No. 22 Dakota Johnson: slightly more high-toned stuff.

films.title: Transformers
films.note: No. 21 Shia LaBeouf: the franchise that made him a household name.

films.title: Honey Boy
films.note: No. 21 Shia LaBeouf.

films.title: The Peanut Butter Falcon
films.note: No. 21 Shia LaBeouf.

films.title: The Tax Collector
films.note: No. 21 Shia LaBeouf: the David Ayer movie so many people in Hollywood have already seen, that nobody's ready to release.

films.title: Room
films.note: No. 20 Brie Larson: the Oscar.

films.title: Captain Marvel
films.note: No. 20 Brie Larson.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: No. 20 Brie Larson.

films.title: The Marvels
films.note: No. 20 Brie Larson: then-titled Captain Marvel 2, next up.

films.title: Booksmart
films.note: No. 19 Beanie Feldstein: a 'sunbeam,' per both hosts, even as the movie itself aged worse than her performance.

films.title: Lady Bird
films.note: No. 19 Beanie Feldstein.

films.title: The Humans
films.note: No. 19 Beanie Feldstein: the Stephen Karam adaptation, alongside Amy Schumer and Richard Jenkins.

films.title: Creed
films.note: No. 18 Michael B. Jordan: one of the best moviegoing experiences either host has had in a decade.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: No. 18 Michael B. Jordan: 'all caps acting,' per Amanda, which works, Sean argues, because that's how comic books actually sound.

films.title: Just Mercy
films.note: No. 18 Michael B. Jordan: the same register applied to a courtroom drama, which both hosts think doesn't land as well.

films.title: Creed II
films.note: No. 18 Michael B. Jordan: didn't land the same way as the first.

films.title: Kingsman: The Secret Service
films.note: No. 17 Taron Egerton.

films.title: Robin Hood
films.note: No. 17 Taron Egerton.

films.title: Rocketman
films.note: No. 17 Taron Egerton: played Elton John, campaigned hard for the Oscar, won a Golden Globe instead.

films.title: The Master
films.note: No. 16 Jesse Plemons.

films.title: Game Night
films.note: No. 16 Jesse Plemons: 'fucking amazing,' one of the funniest performances of the decade, per Sean.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: No. 16 Jesse Plemons: took a tiny part just to be in it.

films.title: I'm Thinking of Ending Things
films.note: No. 16 Jesse Plemons: the Kaufman movie, opposite Jessie Buckley.

films.title: Twilight
films.note: No. 15 Kristen Stewart: the phenomenon that made her, and the paparazzi storm that came with it.

films.title: Panic Room
films.note: No. 15 Kristen Stewart.

films.title: Adventureland
films.note: No. 15 Kristen Stewart: one of Amanda's favorites.

films.title: Personal Shopper
films.note: No. 15 Kristen Stewart: probably the last great movie she's made, per Sean.

films.title: Charlie's Angels
films.note: No. 15 Kristen Stewart: Amanda says she's genuinely funny in it.

films.title: Happiest Season
films.note: No. 15 Kristen Stewart: Clea DuVall's directorial debut, one of the more hotly tipped Oscar movies of the fall.

films.title: Widows
films.note: No. 14 Cynthia Erivo.

films.title: Bad Times at the El Royale
films.note: No. 14 Cynthia Erivo.

films.title: Harriet
films.note: No. 14 Cynthia Erivo.

films.title: Chaos Walking
films.note: No. 14 Cynthia Erivo: the Doug Liman movie both hosts call maybe the most cursed production in recent memory.

films.title: Needle in a Timestack
films.note: No. 14 Cynthia Erivo.

films.title: Wonder Woman
films.note: No. 13 Gal Gadot: a movie Amanda loved.

films.title: Fast Five
films.note: No. 13 Gal Gadot.

films.title: Wonder Woman 1984
films.note: No. 13 Gal Gadot.

films.title: Death on the Nile
films.note: No. 13 Gal Gadot.

films.title: Spider-Man: Far From Home
films.note: No. 12 Zendaya: they finally give her something to do.

films.title: The Greatest Showman
films.note: No. 12 Zendaya: Sean doesn't rate the movie, liked her in it.

films.title: Smallfoot
films.note: No. 12 Zendaya: source of the 'Zendaya is Michi' viral posters.

films.title: Dune
films.note: No. 12 Zendaya: a pretty significant part, per Sean.

films.title: War Dogs
films.note: No. 11 Ana de Armas.

films.title: Blade Runner 2049
films.note: No. 11 Ana de Armas.

films.title: Knives Out
films.note: No. 11 Ana de Armas: the centerpiece, and her real breakout.

films.title: Blonde
films.note: No. 11 Ana de Armas: playing Marilyn Monroe, in Andrew Dominik's adaptation of the Joyce Carol Oates novel.

films.title: No Time to Die
films.note: No. 11 Ana de Armas.

films.title: Deep Water
films.note: No. 11 Ana de Armas: the Adrian Lyne movie, opposite Ben Affleck, where they actually started dating.

films.title: Short Term 12
films.note: No. 10 LaKeith Stanfield.

films.title: Selma
films.note: No. 10 LaKeith Stanfield.

films.title: Sorry to Bother You
films.note: No. 10 LaKeith Stanfield: an early, boundary-pushing choice.

films.title: Get Out
films.note: No. 10 LaKeith Stanfield.

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: No. 10 LaKeith Stanfield.

films.title: Knives Out
films.note: No. 10 LaKeith Stanfield: the metronome holding the movie's showier performances together.

films.title: The Photograph
films.note: No. 10 LaKeith Stanfield: in the middle of a very hot run.

films.title: Winter's Bone
films.note: No. 9 Jennifer Lawrence: the drama that got her on the map in the first place.

films.title: The Hunger Games
films.note: No. 9 Jennifer Lawrence.

films.title: Silver Linings Playbook
films.note: No. 9 Jennifer Lawrence.

films.title: American Hustle
films.note: No. 9 Jennifer Lawrence.

films.title: mother!
films.note: No. 9 Jennifer Lawrence: an amazing experiment, per Sean, not a box-office success.

films.title: Joy
films.note: No. 9 Jennifer Lawrence: the third David O. Russell movie, less successful than the other two.

films.title: X-Men: Apocalypse
films.note: No. 9 Jennifer Lawrence: couldn't save it.

films.title: Passengers
films.note: No. 9 Jennifer Lawrence: couldn't save this one either.

films.title: Red Sparrow
films.note: No. 9 Jennifer Lawrence.

films.title: Dark Phoenix
films.note: No. 9 Jennifer Lawrence: one of the worst movies of the decade, per Sean.

films.title: Causeway
films.note: No. 9 Jennifer Lawrence: then-titled Red, White and Water, an A24/Scott Rudin drama meant to get her back to her Winter's Bone roots.

films.title: Onward
films.note: No. 8 Tom Holland: the voice role.

films.title: The Devil All the Time
films.note: No. 8 Tom Holland: an Antonio Campos movie, no soft or cuddly territory.

films.title: Cherry
films.note: No. 8 Tom Holland: the Russo brothers' adaptation of Nico Walker's opioid-addiction novel, a gnarly ask for Spider-Man's fans.

films.title: Atonement
films.note: No. 7 Saoirse Ronan: one of four Oscar nominations, when she couldn't have been more than 12.

films.title: Hanna
films.note: No. 7 Saoirse Ronan.

films.title: The Grand Budapest Hotel
films.note: No. 7 Saoirse Ronan.

films.title: Brooklyn
films.note: No. 7 Saoirse Ronan.

films.title: Lady Bird
films.note: No. 7 Saoirse Ronan.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: No. 7 Saoirse Ronan: a real box-office hit by mainstream-drama standards.

films.title: The French Dispatch
films.note: No. 7 Saoirse Ronan: one of many stars in it, next.

films.title: Twilight
films.note: No. 6 Robert Pattinson: the teen-idol phase he's spent years trying to extricate himself from.

films.title: The Lighthouse
films.note: No. 6 Robert Pattinson: proof he pulled it off; got the show to promise a dedicated episode.

films.title: The Batman
films.note: No. 6 Robert Pattinson: the reason he's this high is the biggest movie of the following year, if Warner Bros. doesn't screw it up.

films.title: Midsommar
films.note: No. 5 Florence Pugh: fills up the entire screen, per Sean.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: No. 5 Florence Pugh.

films.title: Outlaw King
films.note: No. 5 Florence Pugh: the queen, in an otherwise boring Netflix movie, per Sean.

films.title: Black Widow
films.note: No. 5 Florence Pugh: her Marvel entry, next.

films.title: Get Out
films.note: No. 4 Daniel Kaluuya: made Sean peg him as a future Tom Hanks type.

films.title: Widows
films.note: No. 4 Daniel Kaluuya: recalibrated that read toward John Malkovich.

films.title: Queen & Slim
films.note: No. 4 Daniel Kaluuya: recalibrated it again, toward Don Cheadle; Sean calls it not fully successful but says Kaluuya is great in it.

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: No. 4 Daniel Kaluuya: the then-untitled Fred Hampton project, his only confirmed thing on the slate.

films.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
films.note: No. 3 Margot Robbie: the role that first got her noticed.

films.title: I, Tonya
films.note: No. 3 Margot Robbie: the movie she produced that springboarded her into serious, Oscar-nominated territory.

films.title: Terminal
films.note: No. 3 Margot Robbie: a movie she produced that Sean calls one of the worst he's ever seen.

films.title: Dreamland
films.note: No. 3 Margot Robbie: a movie she produced that basically nobody saw.

films.title: Promising Young Woman
films.note: No. 3 Margot Robbie: a movie she produced, with complicated feelings Amanda promises to revisit.

films.title: Birds of Prey
films.note: No. 3 Margot Robbie: neither host thought this one, which she also produced, worked.

films.title: The French Dispatch
films.note: No. 2 Timothee Chalamet.

films.title: Dune
films.note: No. 2 Timothee Chalamet: what the hosts call his potential Titanic-style breakthrough.

films.title: Superbad
films.note: No. 1 Emma Stone.

films.title: Birdman
films.note: No. 1 Emma Stone.

films.title: The Favourite
films.note: No. 1 Emma Stone: a supporting, chamber-piece performance both hosts loved.

films.title: Aloha
films.note: No. 1 Emma Stone: one of the misses Sean says he's willing to overlook.

films.title: La La Land
films.note: No. 1 Emma Stone: the Oscar win Sean says tips the scales.

films.title: Cruella
films.note: No. 1 Emma Stone: the 101 Dalmatians prequel up next, which Sean doubts will feel like the center of the conversation.
